name: Meilisearch
what_it_does: Open-source, typo-tolerant full-text search engine written in Rust with an official MCP server, offering instant search results, faceting, filtering, and hybrid semantic+keyword search.
use_cases: Adding fast typo-tolerant search to web applications via agent-driven indexing and queries
Hybrid semantic + keyword search combining vector embeddings with BM25
Faceted product search with filters and sorting for e-commerce
Agent-driven document indexing and retrieval workflows
Geosearch and ranking with custom ranking rules
not_for: Log analytics or time-series data (use Elasticsearch or Loki)
Complex aggregation queries requiring SQL-like GROUP BY
Datasets larger than a few hundred million documents (Meilisearch has memory constraints)
best_when: You need instant, developer-friendly full-text search with excellent typo tolerance and want an MCP server to let agents interact with search and indexing directly.
avoid_when: You need log analytics, complex aggregations, or enterprise-scale distributed search.

auth.notes: Master key, admin key, and search-only keys with configurable scopes and expiry. Keys can be scoped to specific indexes and actions. Very flexible key management via API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted is completely free; Meilisearch Cloud free trial available
pricing.notes: MIT-licensed open source. Self-hosting is free. Meilisearch Cloud is the managed offering. Excellent for small to medium scale; free tier is generous for prototyping.

performance.rate_limits: No hard rate limits on self-hosted

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include code, message, and type fields. Error codes are meaningful (e.g., index_not_found, invalid_search_limit). Documentation is excellent with playground.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document upserts are idempotent by primary key. Tasks are asynchronous — task IDs enable polling for completion. Documents with the same primary key are updated in place.
known_agent_gotchas: All write operations (add, update, delete) are asynchronous tasks — agents must poll task status for consistency
Primary key must be consistent across all documents in an index — changing it requires deleting the index
Hybrid search requires embedder configuration at index level — not enabled by default
MCP server requires Meilisearch URL and master key as environment variables
Index settings changes (ranking rules, filterable attributes) trigger full reindexing — avoid in production during queries
Maximum document size is 1MB — large documents must be chunked before indexing
